message:
perf(stdio): strict-hot-path write helper that bypasses runtime-policy gate

Adds write_bytes_without_runtime_policy() and routes fputc /
fputs / fwrite / puts through it on the strict-mode hot path.
The previous implementation ran every byte of user stdio output
through runtime_policy::decide(), which was measurable overhead
(and extra decision-gate allocations) for programs that call
printf / fwrite in tight loops, even though the runtime-policy
ring only wants to observe stdio writes for intrusive scenarios
(ffi-pcc gating, tracing) that explicitly opt in.

crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs:

- New unsafe helper write_bytes_without_runtime_policy(id, stream,
  bytes) that carries through the full stream behaviour the main
  write path cares about (cookie streams, offset bookkeeping,
  is_mem_backed memory streams, host fwrite fallback) while
  skipping the runtime_policy::decide call entirely.
- The cookie-stream arm keeps its StreamPolicyState::Write loop
  (Retry / Yield / Escalate / Flush / Buffer) so EINTR and partial
  writes still get the same treatment they always did, and
  stream.set_error() / set_offset() stay in sync.
- The host-fallback arm marks the stream with mark_host_io_started
  so the subsequent fflush/fclose path still sees the activity.

crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/stdio_abi_test.rs:

- Adds strict_hot_stdio_writes_skip_runtime_policy_gate which opens
  a tmpfile, calls fputc / fputs / fwrite / puts, and asserts
  take_last_decision_gate_for_tests() returns None after each.
  Locks in that the runtime-policy decision gate no longer fires
  on stdio writes when the runtime is marked ready but the strict
  hot path is the one in use.
